mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and business demand, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with a win rate above 50% across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index increase above 0.5% reflects accelerating manufacturing output that boosts short-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases above 0.5% MoM consistently precede negative 5d SPX returns, yielding a Rank IC below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real interest rate compression that erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth alongside stable FEDFUNDS yields positive 20d SPX returns in > 60% of cases, breaking the inflation-valuation link.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60d SPX direction, with Rank IC consistently between -0.02 and 0.02 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction signals an economic recession that immediately triggers equity risk premium repricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, negating the recession repricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that constrain corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5d returns with a win rate above 45% during supply-constrained periods.
